prose:
**Benzene frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO orbital gap. Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**) using PySCF. Geometry: fixed user-supplied XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz` (12 atoms); this was a single-point frontier-orbital calculation, not a geometry optimization. Environment: gas phase. Charge/multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1. SCF convergence: converged in 8 cycles. Warnings: no warnings reported. Engine integrity trustworthy verdict: true. Provenance: computed.

Headline result: **HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 eV**.

Additional orbital values returned by chemkit: HOMO = -7.0468329 eV; LUMO = -0.41574728 eV. Total electronic energy = -232.33724 Hartree (-6322.2183 eV). Koopmans-style quantities returned: vertical IP = 7.0468329 eV; vertical EA = 0.41574728 eV; electronegativity = 3.7312901 eV; chemical hardness = 3.3155428 eV; chemical softness = 0.30160974 eV^-1; electrophilicity index = 2.0995847 eV.

Exact chemkit command reported by the engine: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpaa6h7cmb/live.json`. The chemkit result did not include a live `.out` log path field; the JSON output path in the returned command was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpaa6h7cmb/live.json`.
